Level of theory: DFT tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitting employed) in PySCF 2.x backend.
Geometry provenance: single-point evaluation on the supplied aniline geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz); no geometry optimisation performed (DFT//fixed-input).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) for the N-electron state; cation (+1, doublet) and anion (−1, doublet) states used internally for finite-difference Fukui evaluation.
Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles converged (integrity.status = "ok"); the engine raised three warnings, reproduced verbatim below. Density and charge conservation checks report sums of f+ and f− = 1.0000, within tolerance. No fall-backs were triggered.
